_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L6 'Heavy Mail + Over-Jerkin + First Gold + Longbow Shape' keeper and apply the L6→L7 delta to produce the Lvl 7 'Basic Plate' variant (Tier 3 base — first plate arrives, skullcap evolves into open-face conical helm). Tier-break level via chained input.
_meta.input_image_content: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L6/composite/archer_L6_v<keeper>_composited.png (v3 chain, user picks keeper at runtime)
_meta.level_beat_doc: UnitProgression/Archer/Archer_Progression_Plan.md (L7 — Basic Plate; revised 2026-05-07 per AD FeedbackV0)
_meta.input_strategy: Chained tier-break (single-input chained, locked 2026-05-07) — previous keeper composite is the sole input. No L1_Base scale anchor.
history: v1 (2026-05-05): see prior history — open-face helm replaces hood, breastplate replaces over-jerkin, steel vambraces replace bracers, steel pauldron domes replace studded leather. Short-sleeve scale mail at the bicep gap.
v_no_stubble (2026-05-05): Stripped 'faint stubble shadow'. Canonical face is clean-shaven.

v2_detail_economy (2026-05-07): MAJOR REWRITE per AD FeedbackV0 (CLAUDE.md Part 3 §23 + Part 2 §H/J). (a) HEAD: hood is ALREADY DOWN (since L5), steel skullcap is FULLY VISIBLE on top of head (since L5). At L7 the skullcap EVOLVES into a TALLER OPEN-FACE CONICAL STEEL HELM (taller silhouette beat — cleanly extends the existing skullcap upward into the conical apex). The scale-mail coif at back/sides retires (covered by the new helm extending down to brow + temples). The red MANTLE at the shoulders (from L5) STAYS as the red-at-head/neck anchor — the L7 prompt no longer needs a separate red coif/scarf. (b) FULL-ARM SCALE MAIL (NEW per AD): scale-mail sleeves NOW EXTEND from deltoid all the way to wrist (full bicep-to-wrist coverage — NO MORE short-sleeve + bare-skin band). The new steel vambraces sit ON TOP of the full-arm scale-mail at the forearm. (c) BOW: limbs grow further from ~20%-lengthened (L6) to FULL LONGBOW proportions — wood-brown, brass grip-end bindings + brass tip caps carry over, no gold on the bow at this level. (d) Gold trim migrates from leather bracer wrist edges (retired) to the steel vambrace wrist edges. (e) PRESERVE block updated: dropped hip pouches + tool-loop + strop + sharpening stone (v3 chain has none); dropped leather forehead band (was retired in v3 L4); dropped scale-mail coif at neck (replaced by the helm extending down). (f) Updated paths to v3 chain. Adds 4 → 5 (bow shape evolution = its own beat).

v3_single_input (2026-05-07): Dropped L1_Base from image_urls per locked single-input chained rule (CLAUDE.md Part 1 §B revised). Stripped 'SECOND input image' / scale-anchor language from prompt body and FRAMING block heading. Sole input is the previous composited keeper. urls_before=2, urls_after=1, body_chars_removed=101.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L6/composite/archer_L6_v<keeper>_composited.png>> (CONTENT REFERENCE — the v3 L6 keeper to upgrade)
prompt: CRITICAL — TWO HARD RULES BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE. (A) LIVERY BAND PRESERVATION: the mustard-yellow tunic with the quilted red center panel and quilted red side panels MUST REMAIN VISIBLE as a CLEAR HORIZONTAL BAND, four to six inches tall, between the lower edge of the new steel breastplate and the upper edge of the wider leather belt. The new steel breastplate STOPS AT MID-TORSO (at the bottom of the breastbone) — it does NOT extend down to the waist. The mustard-yellow base and BOTH red panels must be CLEARLY VISIBLE in this band. DO NOT extend the breastplate down to the belt; DO NOT cover the tunic with the breastplate; DO NOT shrink the livery band into a thin strip. (B) FACE FULLY VISIBLE: the new open-face conical steel helm has NO visor and NO face guard. The FACE IS FULLY VISIBLE from forehead to chin — eyes, nose, mouth, jawline all clearly readable. DO NOT add a closed visor; DO NOT add a face guard; DO NOT cover any part of the face with metal. NOW APPLY THE FIVE UPGRADES BELOW WITHOUT VIOLATING RULES A OR B. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE additive upgrades to the archer in the input image (the 'Heavy Mail + Over-Jerkin + First Gold + Longbow Shape' content reference) to produce the 'Basic Plate' variant — first plate arrives, the steel skullcap evolves into a taller open-face conical helm, scale-mail sleeves extend to full-arm coverage, the bow grows to full longbow proportions. KEEP EVERYTHING FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY AS-IS unless explicitly changed by the five upgrades below. ===== NEW ADDS FOR THIS EDIT (first plate arrives) ===== (1) STEEL SKULLCAP EVOLVES INTO A TALLER OPEN-FACE CONICAL STEEL HELM — the simple polished steel skullcap on top of the head in the input image GROWS UPWARD into a taller open-face conical helm. The new helm is the SAME polished cool silver-gray steel as the skullcap (continuous evolution — no material change), but the silhouette extends upward into a low CONICAL APEX above the crown of the head, with the helm rim now sitting at the brow line and curving down past the temples to just behind the ears. The two small painted iron rivets at the temples carry over to the new helm rim. The helm is open-face — NO visor, NO face guard, NO finial, NO spike, NO gold trim, NO engraving, NO skull motif at this level. Clean smooth plate surface only. The face is fully visible from forehead to chin. The scale-mail coif that previously framed the back and sides of the head retires at this level — the new helm extends down past the temples and behind the ears, covering where the coif used to sit. The red CLOTH MANTLE at the shoulders (from the input image) remains in place as the red-at-head/neck anchor — it drapes from the back of the neck across the shoulders just as in the input image, now visible behind the new helm rim at the back of the neck. (2) STEEL BREASTPLATE REPLACES THE LEATHER OVER-JERKIN — the brown leather sleeveless over-jerkin from the input image is GONE. In its place sits a polished cool silver-gray steel breastplate covering the chest from the collarbones down to MID-TORSO (stops at the bottom of the breastbone, NOT extending to the waist). Plain polished plate with a soft top-down gradient and a clean central highlight running vertically; no engraving, no rivets, no gold trim. Smooth single-piece curved silhouette with subtle pectoral contouring. The leather throat collar from the input image is GONE — the breastplate's upper edge now meets the base of the helm rim at the neck. The breastplate STOPS at mid-torso so the mustard-yellow tunic with the quilted red center panel and quilted red side panels remains CLEARLY VISIBLE as a horizontal livery band (4-6 inches tall) between the breastplate's lower edge and the wider leather belt. (3) FULL-ARM SCALE MAIL + STEEL VAMBRACES REPLACE LEATHER BRACERS (NO BICEP GAP) — the scale-mail sleeves on both arms in the input image are SHORT (deltoid + upper bicep, mid-bicep scallop edge, with a bare-skin band visible from mid-bicep down to the leather wrist bracer at mid-forearm). In this edit, the SCALE MAIL ON BOTH ARMS NOW EXTENDS FROM THE DELTOID ALL THE WAY DOWN TO THE WRIST — covering the bicep + elbow + upper forearm + lower forearm in continuous chunky overlapping cool silver-gray scale plates (same chunky-scale style as the existing sleeves and body band). NO BARE-SKIN BAND remains on either arm — the scale mail is full-arm coverage. ON TOP of the new full-arm scale mail at the forearm, both forearms now wear tapered cool silver-gray STEEL VAMBRACES, each running from the wrist up to just below the elbow with a single longitudinal seam visible along the outer side of each vambrace, polished steel with a soft top-down highlight, slightly flared at the elbow end. The vambrace sits OVER the full-arm scale mail at the forearm region — the scale mail is visible at the bicep + elbow above the vambrace's upper edge, and the vambrace is visible at the forearm. A thin BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD trim line runs around the WRIST EDGE of each vambrace (the gold trim from the leather bracers in the input image migrates here — saturated warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the cool silver-gray steel, NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint; render as: saturated buttery-yellow base + soft top-down highlight reading almost white-yellow + deeper warm shadow on the underside reading honey-orange — IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale). The leather bracers from the input image are GONE — both forearms wear steel vambraces. The hands themselves stay BARE peachy-tan skin (no gauntlets, no gloves). (4) SINGLE-PIECE STEEL PAULDRON DOMES REPLACE THE PLAIN LEATHER PAULDRON CAPS — both shoulders wear a smooth dome-shape single-piece steel pauldron cap in the same cool silver-gray polished steel as the helm and breastplate, with a bright leading-edge highlight along the front rim. Each pauldron is a single solid plate (NOT layered, NOT segmented into multiple lames, NO rivets, NO gold trim — single-piece dome only), capping the very top of each deltoid and curving down to about the upper-bicep level over the new full-arm scale mail. The plain leather pauldron caps from the input image are GONE — both shoulders wear single-piece steel pauldron domes. (5) BOW UPGRADE — LIMBS GROW TO FULL LONGBOW PROPORTIONS — the bow in the input image has limbs that are ~20% lengthened from the original short recurve. In this edit each limb (BOTH the upper limb and the lower limb) GROWS FURTHER, evolving the bow to FULL LONGBOW PROPORTIONS with each limb roughly 1.5Ã— the length they had in the input image. The recurve curvature flattens almost entirely with the new length — the bow now reads as a tall straight-limbed longbow with only a slight residual curve at the limb tips. The grip stays at the SAME hip-level position in the bow-arm hand (the hand and grip do not move); only the limbs grow longer. The new total bow length spans from roughly upper-chest level on the upper side down past the upper thigh on the lower side. The brass grip-end binding rings, the brass tip caps at both limb ends, and the warm wood-brown limb material all carry over to the lengthened bow — the new longer wood between the brass details remains warm wood-brown. NO GOLD anywhere on the bow at this level — the bow stays brass + wood-brown. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== Every other element of the input image must remain identical: face (chiseled chibi jawline + clean-shaven smooth peachy-tan skin + closed-mouth half-smirk + asymmetric brow with anatomical-LEFT brow higher + warm brown eyes with single catchlight + small upturned nose); the red CLOTH MANTLE at the shoulders / back of the neck (carried over from the input image as the red-at-head/neck anchor — now visible behind the new helm rim at the back of the neck); matching crimson-red cape attached at both shoulders falling to knee length with soft folds and inward curl on viewer-LEFT; both hands BARE peachy-tan; bare anatomical-LEFT hand gripping the bow grip vertically at hip height (the grip stays at the same hip-level position even with the lengthened longbow limbs); the bow with warm wood-brown limbs (now full longbow length per add 5) + dark-brown leather grip wrap + brass grip-end binding rings + brass tip caps + pale cream bowstring; brown leather cylindrical back-quiver over anatomical-LEFT shoulder rising behind viewer-RIGHT side of head with three to four white-feather red-tip arrows; mustard-yellow tunic with quilted red center panel and quilted red side panels (visible as a clear horizontal livery band between the breastplate and belt — see Rule A); leather X-strap chest harness diagonally across the front from anatomical-LEFT shoulder to anatomical-RIGHT hip with brass ring at the crossing, sitting OVER the new steel breastplate; wider single warm-brown leather belt with brass rectangular buckle and visible edge stitching, sitting at the natural waist below the livery band; dark-wood dagger in leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip with brass capstone on handle (STANDALONE — no hip pouches, no satchels, no tool-loop rings accompany it); brown leather greaves on lower legs from boot cuff to just below knee with single cross-strap binding each; olive forest-green knee-length trousers (visible above the greaves); chunky warm-brown leather boots with the darker folded ankle cuff — including the SMALL STEEL TOE CAP wrapping the front toe of each boot (carried over from the previous level — the toe cap stays UNCHANGED at this level). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately 15-25 degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE BOW ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) hangs DOWN at his side with elbow only slightly bent, bow held VERTICALLY at HIP height in front of the hip. THE DRAW ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) bent at roughly 90 degrees, FOREARM HORIZONTAL ACROSS THE CHEST at upper-chest level, vambraced hand at chest CENTER in TIGHT CLOSED FIST with KNUCKLES OUTWARD. Calm balanced HERO STANCE; NOT an active draw. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character at ~75% vertical fill (~765px tall), ~57% horizontal width (~580px wide). TOP of helm conical apex at ~13.5% from top of canvas (the new helm extends slightly higher than the previous skullcap; the conical apex sits at the canonical top-margin position). BOTTOM of boots at ~12% from bottom of canvas. Character roughly vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. The full-longbow limbs may extend slightly beyond the body silhouette but should not push the canvas top or bottom margins — the bow grip stays at hip level so the limbs grow into the existing torso/upper-leg space. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadow recesses, slight thinning under the key light. Interior linework soft and value-driven, never hard black. Clean white background, no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT pull a cloth hood back up — the cloth hood retired two levels ago and stays DOWN at the shoulders as the red mantle. DO NOT keep the simple steel skullcap shape — the skullcap GROWS UPWARD into a taller open-face conical helm; the silhouette is taller, with a low conical apex above the crown. DO NOT add a closed visor on the helm; DO NOT add a face guard; DO NOT cover any part of the face with metal. DO NOT add a finial, spike, crown, horn, plume, or any decorative element to the helm at this level. DO NOT engrave the helm or add rivets at the rim other than the two iron rivets at the temples carried over from the previous level. DO NOT add gold trim or gold band to the helm — the helm is plain polished steel only at this level. DO NOT add a skull motif or any bone imagery. DO NOT keep the scale-mail coif at the back/sides of the head — the new helm covers that area; the coif retires at this level. DO NOT extend the steel breastplate down to the belt — the breastplate STOPS at mid-torso so the mustard-yellow tunic livery band remains visible (Rule A). DO NOT cover the tunic with the breastplate; DO NOT shrink the livery band into a thin strip. DO NOT replace the tunic with a steel coat of plates. DO NOT add gold trim, gold band, or gold accents to the breastplate — only the thin gold trim line at the new vambraces' wrist edges. DO NOT keep the brown leather over-jerkin — it is REPLACED by the steel breastplate. DO NOT keep the leather throat collar — it is GONE. DO NOT keep either leather bracer — both are REPLACED by steel vambraces. DO NOT add gauntlets or metal gloves — hands stay BARE peachy-tan. DO NOT keep the plain leather pauldron caps — they are REPLACED by single-piece steel pauldron domes. DO NOT segment the steel pauldrons into multiple lames; DO NOT add rivets or gold trim to the steel pauldron domes — single-piece smooth plate only at this level. DO NOT leave the bare-skin bicep + elbow + upper-forearm band visible — at this level the scale mail extends FULL bicep-to-wrist (NO bicep gap); the steel vambrace sits ON TOP of the scale mail at the forearm. DO NOT shorten the scale-mail sleeves back to deltoid+upper-bicep — at this level the sleeves are FULL-arm coverage from deltoid all the way to wrist. DO NOT add tassets or arming-skirt strips at the hip. DO NOT add a closed-front cuirass extending to the waist. DO NOT add a fauld plate skirt. DO NOT add ANY hip pouch, belt pouch, leather satchel, tool-loop ring, or hip-mounted accessory — the dagger sheath is the ONLY belt accessory at this level. DO NOT add gold to the bow — at this level the bow stays brass + wood-brown only (gilt limb tips arrive at later level). DO NOT shorten the bow back to short-recurve or ~20%-lengthened — at this level the bow is at FULL LONGBOW proportions, ~1.5Ã— the limb length from the previous level. DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, or engraved patterns. DO NOT swap the hand holding the bow. DO NOT change the chibi proportions. DO NOT change any facial feature, hair, or skin tone. DO NOT change the cape color, length, or drape. DO NOT change the arrow fletching colors (white feather + red tips). DO NOT add or remove arrows in the back-quiver. DO NOT change the back-quiver position. DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition. DO NOT crop the figure. DO NOT change the background or add scenery. DO NOT drift the art style. DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
